name: Izanagi and Izanami
shortDescription: Izanagi and Izanami are the primordial deities in Shinto mythology who created the Japanese islands and many of the other gods.
description: Izanagi and Izanami are central figures in the creation myth of Japan. Tasked by the heavenly deities with creating the land, they used the Amenonuhoko (heavenly jeweled spear) to stir the primordial ocean. As Izanagi withdrew the spear, brine dripped from its tip, forming Onogoro Island. Descending to this island, they built a palace and performed a ritual to become husband and wife. Their initial attempts at procreation were flawed, attributed to Izanami speaking first during the ritual. Correcting this error, they successfully gave birth to the Japanese islands, starting with Awaji Island and continuing with other major islands. Izanami later died giving birth to Kagutsuchi, the fire god. Heartbroken, Izanagi journeyed to Yomi (the underworld) to retrieve her, but was horrified by her decaying form. He fled, sealing the entrance to Yomi, which led to Izanami vowing to kill a thousand people each day, to which Izanagi responded by vowing to create fifteen hundred. This myth explains the origin of death and the cycle of life and death in Shinto beliefs. Izanagi then purified himself in a river, from which Amaterasu (sun goddess), Tsukuyomi (moon god), and Susanoo (storm god) were born.
quests: Journey to Yomi to retrieve Izanami
Purification and birth of major deities
